channel.title: World - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: We recreated the legendary heatwave summer of 1976 in today’s climate – here’s what we found - The Conversation
item.description:

  5. We would like to hear your memories of the 1976 UK heatwave  The Guardian

item.title: Putin told his cabinet that Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil refineries are having no effect on the war. He said this at a meeting called to address the fuel crisis those strikes helped create. - Meduza
item.description:
1. Putin told his cabinet that Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil refineries are having no effect on the war. He said this at a meeting called to address the fuel crisis those strikes helped create.  Meduza
  2. Ukraine war briefing: Crimea locks down as Putin acknowledges ‘huge stream’ of Ukrainian drones  The Guardian
  3. Putin says Ukrainian infrastructure attacks are an attempt to destabilise Russia  Reuters
  4. Poland Warns Putin May Stage False-Flag Attack as Pretext for Escalation, Invokes 1939 Nazi Tactic  Kyiv Post
  5. Putin Accuses Ukraine of Targeting Russia’s Energy Infrastructure  8am.media

item.title: Blink and you’ll miss it: This spider’s spring-loaded trap accelerates faster than a Formula One race car - CNN
item.description:
1. Blink and you’ll miss it: This spider’s spring-loaded trap accelerates faster than a Formula One race car  CNN
  2. Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia  BBC
  3. Newly Discovered Spider Is Basically A Mechanical Engineer, Building Spring-Loaded Snares To Catapult Its Prey  IFLScience
  4. New-to-science spider builds trap that flings ants into the air  New Scientist
  5. Newly discovered spider uses 'ballista' web to capture prey  Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: Seismic waves bounced off Earth’s core and shifted Japan after massive 2011 earthquake - CNN
item.description:
1. Seismic waves bounced off Earth’s core and shifted Japan after massive 2011 earthquake  CNN
  2. An earthquake hit Japan so hard it made the entire country move  Scientific American
  3. A seismic wave from Japan's 2011 magnitude-9 earthquake travelled nearly 2,900 kilometres down to Earth's core, bounced back to the surface 13 minutes later, and shifted the entire country eastward by roughly six millimetres at the same instant — in the first  Space Daily
  4. Scientists Propose Black Holes Don’t Exist, Are Something Much Stranger  404 Media
  5. Japan was shifted eastward by a few millimetres, researchers believe  Yahoo

item.title: Lukashenko Strives To Keep Belarus Out Of War After Zelenskyy Ultimatum - Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty
item.description:

  3. Kremlin accuses Ukraine of threatening sovereignty of close Russian ally Belarus  Reuters
  4. Kremlin Calls Zelensky’s Ultimatum to Lukashenko Aggressive After One-Week Deadline  Kyiv Post
  5. Belarus halts equipment used to guide Russian strikes, Zelensky says, after Ukraine's ultimatum  The Kyiv Independent
